name: Bon Bons
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Betty Crocker.
DESCRIPTION: Daintly little bon-bons, colorfully frosted, each with a surprise inside! These will dress up any cookie box.
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Mix butter, sugar, and vanilla thoroughly until the mixture is smooth.
3. Gradually work in flour and salt until the dough holds together. If the batter is too crumbly, add up to two tablespoons of milk or cream.
4. Divide the dough into four equal portions. Each portion should make one dozen cookies.
5. Mold each portion of dough by spoonfuls around each of the following (one dozen of each):
- Half an Andes mint
- One Maraschino cherry
- Three chocolate chips (or your favorite semi-sweet chocolate)
- One pecan
6. Mark the cookies with their filling type before baking.
7. Bake for 12-15 minutes or until set but not brown.
Icing:
1. Mix together sugar and cream until smooth.
2. Divide icing into four separate bowls.
3. Use food coloring to create three different colors: pink, green, and yellow.
4. Add the remaining chocolate chips to one bowl of icing and microwave until melted. Mix well.
5. Frost the baked cookies by dipping them into the colored icings:
- Andes mint: Green
- Maraschino cherry: Pink
- Pecan: Yellow
- Chocolate chips: Dark brown
These freeze relatively well for about 1 month. After that, the quality of the centers may diminish.
